questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
In a YDSE setup, we plot the phase difference $(\phi)$ between both waves at point $P$ on the screen against the angular position of point $P$ on the screen. The graph is as shown below.
Choose the correct statement(s)
options: There are a total of 4 minima on the screen
The first maxima above the centre is at $\theta=\frac{\pi}{4}$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{2 \pi}{\lambda}(d \sin \theta)=\phi \\ & 4 \pi=\frac{2 \pi}{\lambda} d \sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right) \\ & d=2 \lambda\end{aligned}$
So, there will be four minima on screen corresponding to path difference of $-\frac{3 \lambda}{2},-\frac{\lambda}{2},+\frac{\lambda}{2}$ and $+\frac{3 \lambda}{2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Consider the situation shown in the figure. Two slits $S_1$ and $S_2$ are placed symmetrically about the line OP which is perpendicular to screen. The space between screen and slits is filled with a liquid of refractive index $\mu_3$. A plate of thickness $t$ and refractive index $\mu_2$ is placed in front of one of the slit. A source $S$ is placed above OP at a distance d . Choose the correct alternatives. $\left[\left(\right.\right.$ Given that $\left.\mathrm{D}=1 \mathrm{~m}, \mathrm{~d}=2 \mathrm{~mm}, \mathrm{t}=6 \times 10^{-6} \mathrm{~m}, \mu_2=1.2, \mu_3=1.8\right]$
options: Position of central maxima from point $P$ is 2 mm
Position of central maxima from point P is $\frac{2}{9} \mathrm{~mm}$
Thickness of glass slab so that central maxima forms at point $P$ is $\frac{20}{3} \times 10^{-6} \mathrm{~m}$
If glass slab is removed, the central maxima shifts by a distance of 2 mm
questions.solution:
(i) Path difference in air is $\Delta x=\frac{d^2}{D}+\left(\mu_2-\mu_3\right) t+\frac{\mu_3 y d}{2 D}$
for position of central maxima $\Delta \mathrm{x}=0 \Rightarrow y=\frac{2}{9} \mathrm{~mm}$
(ii) Thickness of the slab so that central maxima forms at point P
$\Rightarrow t\left(\mu_2-\mu_3\right)+\frac{d^2}{D}=0 \Rightarrow t=\frac{\left(2 \times 10^{-3}\right)^2}{0.6}=\frac{20}{3} \times 10^{-6} \mathrm{~m}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A Young's double slit experiment is conducted in water $\left(\mu_1\right)$ as shown in the figure, and a glass plate of thickness $t$ and refractive index $\mu_2$ is placed in the path of $\mathrm{S}_2$. Then,
options: $\text { Optical path difference at ' } O \text { ' is }\left(\frac{\mu_2}{\mu_1}-1\right) \mathrm{t} \text {. }$
$\text { Optical path difference at ' } O \text { ' is }\left(\mu_2-\mu_1\right) \mathrm{t} \text {.. }$
Optical path difference is independent of position of glass plate, provided light from $S_2$ passes through it.
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
&\text { Equivalent length of the glass plate in water }=\frac{\mu_2 t}{\mu_1}\\
&\therefore \quad \text { O.P.D. }=\left(\frac{\mu_2}{\mu_1}-1\right)
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
A thin paper of thickness 0.02 mm having refractive index 1.45 is pasted across one of the slits in a Young's double slit experiment. The paper transmits $4 / 9$ of light falling on it.
$\left(\lambda_{\text {light }}=600 \mathrm{~nm}\right)$
options: Amplitude of light wave transmitted through the paper will be $2 / 3$ times that of incident wave.
The ratio of maximum and minimum intensity in the fringe pattern will be 25 .
The total number of fringe crossing the centre if an identical paper is pasted on the other slit is 15 .
The ratio of maximum and minimum intensity in the pattern will be 5 .
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{I_{\max }}{I_{\min }}=\frac{\left(a_1+a_2\right)^2}{\left(a_1-a_2\right)^2} \\ & \frac{a_1}{a_2}=\sqrt{\frac{l_1}{I_2}}=\sqrt{\frac{4}{9}}=\frac{2}{3} \\ & \therefore \frac{I_{\max }}{I_{\min }}=25\end{aligned}$
If an identical paper is pasted across second slit shifted C.B.F. will back in central point.
shift $=(\mu-1) \frac{\mathrm{tD}}{\mathrm{d}}$
fringe width $=\frac{\mathrm{n} \lambda \mathrm{D}}{\mathrm{d}}$
$\therefore \mathrm{n}=\frac{\text { shift }}{\text { fringe width }}=\frac{(\mu-1) \mathrm{t}}{\lambda}=15$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The point $S$ is monochromatic source of light emitting light of wavelength $\lambda$. At the point $P$ at a distance x from the mirror as shown in the figure, interference takes place between two light rays one directly coming from source $S$ and another after reflection from the mirror such that a maxima is formed. The value of $x$ may be
questions.solution:
$\sqrt{(500 x)^2+9 x^2}-\sqrt{(500 x)^2+x^2}=\frac{(2 n+1) \lambda}{2}$
$500 x\left[1+\frac{9}{2 \times 500^2}\right]-500 x\left[1+\frac{1}{2 \times 500^2}\right]=\frac{(2 n+1) \lambda}{2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In an interference arrangement, similar to Young's double-slit experiment, the slits $S_1$ and $S_2$ are illuminated with coherent microwave sources, each of frequency $10^6 \mathrm{~Hz}$. The sources are synchronized to have zero phase difference. The slits are separated by distance $d=150$ m . The intensity $\mathrm{I}_\theta$ is measured as a function of $\theta$, where $\theta$ is defined as shown in figure. If $I_0$ is maximum intensity, then $I_{(\theta)}$ for $0 \leq \theta \leq 90^{\circ}$ is given by
options: $I_{(\theta)}$ is constant for all values of $\theta$
questions.solution: For microwaves, $\mathrm{c}=\mathrm{f} \lambda \Rightarrow \lambda=\frac{\mathrm{c}}{\mathrm{f}}=\frac{3 \times 10^8}{10^6}=300 \mathrm{~m}$ Also, $\Delta \mathrm{x}=\mathrm{d} \sin \theta, \Rightarrow \phi=\frac{2 \pi}{\lambda} \quad \Delta \mathrm{x}=\frac{2 \pi}{\lambda}(\mathrm{~d} \sin \theta)=\frac{2 \pi}{300}(150 \sin \theta)=\pi \sin \theta$ So, $I=I_1+I_2+2\left(\sqrt{I_1 I_2}\right) \cos \phi$ with $I_1=I_2$ and $\phi=\pi \sin \theta$, above equation reduces to $I_R=2 I_1[1+\cos (\pi \sin \theta)]=4 I_1 \cos ^2\left(\frac{\pi \sin \theta}{2}\right)$ As $I_R$ will be maximum when $\cos ^2[(\pi \sin \theta) / 2]$ is maximum, i.e., equal to 1 , so $\left(I_R\right)_{\max }=4 I_1=I_0$ and hence $I=I_0 \cos ^2[(\pi \sin \theta) / 2]$ If $\theta=0^{\circ}, I=I_0 \cos 0^{\circ}=I_0 \quad$ If $\theta=30^{\circ}, I=I_0 \cos ^2\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)=\frac{I_0}{2} \quad$ If $\theta=90^{\circ}, I=I_0 \cos ^2\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In a YDSE set up the separation between the slits is $3 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~m}$. The distance between the slits and the screen is 1.5 m . Light of wavelength in the visible range ( $4000 Å-8000 Å$ ) is allowed to fall on the slits. The wave length in the visible region that will be present on the screen at a distance of $1.5 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~m}$ from central maxima is
questions.solution:
Here the position $y$ on the screen will correspond to maxima.
$$
\begin{aligned}
& y=\frac{n \lambda D}{d} \\
& \lambda=\frac{30,000}{n} \text { when } n=1,2,3,4,\ldots
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The point $S$ is monochromatic source of light emitting light of wavelength $\lambda$. At the point $P$ at a distance $x$ from the mirror as shown in the figure, interference takes place between two light rays one directly coming from source $S$ and another after reflection from the mirror such that a maxima is formed. The value of $x$ may be:
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \Delta x=(S A+A P)-S P \\ & =(S A+A B)-S P \\ & =S B-S P\end{aligned}$ $=\sqrt{(500 x)^2+(3 x)^2}-\sqrt{(500 x)^2+x^2}$
$\begin{aligned} & =500 x\left[\sqrt{1+\frac{9}{500^2}}-\sqrt{1+\frac{1}{500^2}}\right] \\ & =500 x\left[\left(1+\frac{9}{2 \times 500^2}\right)-\left(1+\frac{1}{2 \times 500^2}\right)\right]\end{aligned}$ $=\frac{500 x}{2 \times 500^2}[8]=\frac{\lambda}{2}, \frac{3 \lambda}{2} \ldots$ $x=62.5 \lambda, 187.5 \lambda \ldots \ldots$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
